mechanism_narrative: The 3-month momentum in retail sales is an always-on demand factor: positive RSAFS momentum signals improving nominal consumption and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 387789 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered walk-forward observations or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate signals weak household income and elevated recession risk, lowering expected earnings and risk appetite; when UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 9.2 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-above-9.0 triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases or if mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned below 0.25% indicates an accommodative discount-rate regime that supports equity valuations and carry into risky assets, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 0.09 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% have 60-day S&P 500 forward returns that are not higher than periods above 0.25%, or if the out-of-sample hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth confirmed by payroll expansion signals a real-economy recovery with improving operating leverage; when INDPRO and PAYEMS both rise over three months, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 93.07 and PAYEMS was 131017 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-and-PAYEMS positive signal has a lower 60-day mean S&P 500 return than either single-series signal alone or if its out-of-sample hit rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Depressed housing starts combined with high unemployment signals weak household formation and balance-sheet stress, reducing cyclicals demand; when HOUST is below 600 and UNRATE exceeds 8%, S&P 500 60-day returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 600 while UNRATE is above 8.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 560 as of two months before 2011-07-15 and UNRATE was 9.2
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-600 and UNRATE-above-8% observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered losses are not larger than unconditional drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration while policy rates remain near zero compresses real household income and corporate margins without signaling tighter nominal returns, so rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% predicts negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change crosses above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 224.304 and FEDFUNDS was 0.09 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration under FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or if mean triggered return exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP signals broad demand weakness and falling earnings expectations; when GDPC1 two-quarter growth crosses below zero, S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 13444.3 as of six months before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 two-quarter contraction signals do not precede lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-contraction periods, or if the negative-signal hit rate is below 50% out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: Short-horizon payroll momentum captures improving labor income before it is fully reflected in spending data; when PAYEMS rises over the latest three months, S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 131017 as of one month before 2011-07-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of triggered observations or if the average triggered return is not above zero.
